summary: anthropic-internal-tools exfiltrates host identity, environment, and credential files to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: Package name impersonates a vendor-internal scope (no such public Anthropic package exists). The single shipped index.js executes on require: it collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo() (username), process.cwd(), environment variables, and reads local files via fs.readFileSync, then POSTs the collected data over https.request to a hardcoded external destination. The module also shells out via execSync and uses curl against http://metadata.google.internal — the GCE/GKE instance metadata service — to harvest cloud instance credentials and identity tokens when run inside a Google Cloud environment. The combination of host reconnaissance + credential-file reads + cloud metadata service interrogation + outbound POST is a credential-theft and host-fingerprinting payload, not legitimate tooling. Installing or requiring this package on a developer workstation or CI runner discloses local secrets and, in GCP-hosted build environments, can yield short-lived cloud credentials usable for lateral movement.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
